human_verification.notes: Reviewed ambulatory referral boundaries, source quality, functionality coverage, scenario mapping, privacy controls, and approval-boundary handling for planning-only validated behavior.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest sets explicit ambulatory referral boundaries, required artifacts, output shapes, and refusal or handoff triggers.
Functionality map declares shared, planning-lane, and industry-specific abilities with complete scenario coverage.
Research summary ties AHRQ, ASTP or ONC, CMS, and HHS sources to workflow stages, systems of record, failure modes, and scenario families.

Evaluation scenarios cover capacity constraints, conflicting priorities, approval requests outside lane authority, privacy escalation, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and specialist-decline refresh handling.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain for validated deployment; tracked ambiguities are runtime retrieval dependencies.
Functionality coverage is complete with no uncovered required abilities.
This package remains specification-based and does not claim final clinical, payer, or operational approval authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation requires runtime telemetry, connector reliability across referral systems, and repeated tenant-safe validation evidence.
Tenant policy and retrieval dependencies are required to resolve payer, specialty, and jurisdiction-specific variability at runtime.
